info.title: Rebilly Checkout API
info.description:
# Introduction
This page is about our hosted checkout pages feature.

# Authentication
When you sign up for an account, you are given your first API key.
You can generate additional API keys, and delete API keys (as you may
need to rotate your keys in the future). You authenticate to the
Rebilly API by providing your secret key in the request header.

Rebilly offers three forms of authentication:  private key, JSON Web Tokens, and
public key.
- private key: authenticates each request by searching for the presence
of an HTTP header: REB-APIKEY.
- JWT: authenticates each request by the HTTP header: Authorization.
- public key: authenticates by the HTTP header: REB-AUTH (read more on this below).

Rebilly also offers JSON Web Tokens (JWT) authentication, where you can control
the specific granular permissions and expiration for that JWT.  We call our resource
for generating JWT Sessions.

Never share your secret keys. Keep them guarded and secure.

<!-- ReDoc-Inject: <security-definitions> -->

tags.name: Checkout Pages
tags.description:
Hosted checkout pages can be configured here.  You can customize the theme
and content and more that appears on your hosted checkout pages.

ApiKey.description:
When you sign up for an account, you are given your first API key.
To do so please follow this link: https://www.rebilly.com/site/signup/
Also you can generate additional API keys, and delete API keys (as you may
need to rotate your keys in the future).
ApiKey.name: REB-APIKEY

JWT.description: You can create a JSON Web Token (JWT) via our Sessions resource.

RebAuth.description: Only for the Tokens resource. The REB-AUTH value is created in this way Generate a Nonce (random string), and a Timestamp (unix timestamp) Concatenate the values of ApiUser, Nonce, and Timestamp (in that order). Use that value as the input data when you calculate the HMAC-SHA1 with your secret key, called the signature. Forming the four data points into a JSON object. ```json { "REB-APIUSER": "abcdefg", "REB-NONCE": "adfsjtreitou345fgkdafgj", "REB-TIMESTAMP": "1382591345", "REB-SIGNATURE": "a234fc95460401cfg09c9d09" } ``` Then base64 encode that JSON object. This is the final value of the REB-AUTH HTTP header
RebAuth.name: REB-AUTH

AccessForbidden.description: Access forbidden, invalid API-KEY was used

collectionFilter.name: filter
collectionFilter.description:
The collection items filter requires a special format.
Use "," for multiple allowed values.  Use ";" for multiple fields.
See the filter guide for more options and examples about this format.

collectionQuery.name: q
collectionQuery.description: The partial search of the text fields.

collectionFields.name: fields
collectionFields.description: Limit the returned fields to the list specified, separated by comma.  Note that id is always returned.

collectionExpand.name: expand
collectionExpand.description: Expand response to get full related object intead of ID.  See the expand guide for more info.

get.summary: Retrieve a list of checkout pages

200.description: A list of checkout pages was retrieved successfully

Rate-Limit-Limit.description: The number of allowed requests in the current period

Rate-Limit-Remaining.description: The number of remaining requests in the current period

Rate-Limit-Reset.description:

when the current period will reset

planId.description: Checkout page plan ID.  If the plan is deactivated, the submit button on the checkout page will be deactivated and the page will display "Sold Out."

uriPath.description: Your own custom uri path for this Checkout Page. It will be appended to checkout url https://checkout.rebilly.com/checkout/<uriPath>

name.description: Checkout page name, used for internal display to help you organize your various checkout pages.

isActive.description: If checkout page active.  If deactivated, the checkout page uri will result in a 404.

redirectUrl.description: After a successful checkout, the customer can be redirected to a url of your choice.  If left empty, the customer will remain on the thank you page.

redirectTimeout.description: The time (in seconds) that the customer will remain on the thank you page before being redirected to the `redirectUrl`.

allowCustomCustomerId.description: Set to true if you want to supply your own customer ID's in a querystring parameter on your checkout page.

theme.description: Select from pre-designed themes for your checkout page.

logoUri.description: Secure uri for logo.  Recommended to be a .png, .jpg, or .jpeg under 120KB.  You can reference a file public permalink.  If no logo, your website name will appear at the top of the page.

headerBackgroundColor.description: HEX color of the background of the header section of the page, starts with `#`.

sections.description: Sections of background color, font color and content.

backgroundColor.description: HEX color of the background color for the section, starts with `#`.

fontColor.description: HEX color of the font color, starts with `#`.

content.description: The text you want to appear in the section, written in [markdown](https://github.com/adam-p/markdown-here/wiki/Markdown-Cheatsheet).

formFieldCompanyName.description: Choose whether a field is hidden, optional, or required.

formFieldPhoneNumber.description: Choose whether a field is hidden, optional, or required.

formFieldAddressLine2.description: Choose whether a field is hidden, optional, or required.

formFieldCouponCode.description: Choose whether a field is hidden, optional, or required.

formFieldQuantity.description: Choose whether a field is hidden, optional, or required.  If optional or required, the default value is 1.

formButtonColor.description: HEX color of the form button color, starts with `#`.

formButtonText.description: Text you want to appear on your "Submit" button.

thankYouText.description: A message to display on our hosted "Thank you" page.

ResourceId.description: The resource ID. Defaults to UUID v4
